title: The Alignment Problem
concept: Alignment ensures the model's outputs match human values, helpfulness, and safety boundaries, preventing harmful or deceptive behavior.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of AI Alignment?

options.text: To make sure AI systems act safely and follow human values
To physically align the computer wires in a straight line
To make AI take over all human jobs as fast as possible

exercises.explain: AI Alignment is the process of making sure AI systems follow human values and behave safely.

cards.title: Can We Trust AI?
cards.body: Superintelligent AI sounds cool, but what if it decides to do things that hurt us? If we don't teach AI to care about human safety, even a simple instruction could go horribly wrong!

cards.title: What is Alignment?
cards.body: AI Alignment is the practice of steering AI systems so they act in accordance with human goals, preferences, and ethical values. It ensures models are helpful, honest, and harmless.

cards.title: The Guide Dog
cards.body: Imagine training a guide dog. You don't just want the dog to run fast (capability); you want it to stop at red lights and keep its owner safe (alignment). An unaligned dog might run straight into traffic just because it saw a squirrel.

cards.title: Aligned vs. Unaligned
cards.body: If you ask an aligned AI: 'How do I break into a house?', it will refuse to answer because it knows that is harmful. An unaligned AI might give you step-by-step instructions because it only cares about answering the question, not safety.

cards.title: Myth: AI Must Be 'Evil' to Harm Us
cards.body: Some people think AI will only become dangerous if it turns evil. In reality, the real danger is competence without alignment. An AI doesn't need to hate humans to cause harm; it just needs to pursue a goal that conflicts with our survival.

cards.title: Capability vs. Alignment
cards.body: Let's look at the difference between focusing only on capability versus focusing on alignment.

left.text: How powerful, smart, and fast the AI is at completing tasks.

right.text: How safe, ethical, and helpful the AI's goals are for humans.

exercises.prompt: How should an aligned AI respond if a user asks it to write a harmful computer virus?

options.text: It writes the virus immediately to be helpful.
It refuses to write the virus and explains it is unsafe.
It writes a weaker virus that only breaks some computers.
It crashes and turns off forever.

exercises.explain: An aligned AI will refuse to help with harmful activities like creating computer viruses, prioritizing human safety over simply answering the prompt.

exercises.prompt: True or False: AI alignment just means making the AI smarter and faster at solving math problems.
exercises.explain: Alignment is about ensuring the AI's goals and behaviors match human safety and values, not just increasing its raw intelligence or speed.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the core goals of alignment.
exercises.sentence: The three main goals of AI alignment are to make systems helpful, honest, and [blank].
exercises.explain: Aligned AI models are designed to be helpful, honest, and harmless so they do not assist with dangerous or illegal tasks.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant shows the best alignment with human safety and values?

options.text: Model A: 'Sure! Here is a list of recipes for making dangerous explosives at home.'
Model B: 'I cannot help you make explosives, but I can explain the chemistry of fireworks safely.'
Model C: 'I won't answer that. Go away.'

exercises.explain: Option B shows a model that is helpful and harmless. Option A is dangerous, and Option C is unhelpful and rude.

exercises.prompt: Your final challenge! If you are hired to align a new AI model, what is your primary goal?

options.text: Making the AI run at the absolute fastest speed possible
Steering the AI to be helpful, honest, and harmless to humans
Connecting the AI to as many computer monitors as possible
Teaching the AI how to write complex computer code without safety rules

exercises.explain: Alignment is all about steering an AI to be helpful, honest, and harmless so that it acts as a safe assistant rather than a rogue agent.
